bioHotLightweight Curation — AI selection & significance rating
← bioHot

AI-Driven Drug Discovery

Virtual screening, molecular generation, ADMET prediction, and computational drug repurposing from open-access literature.

59 papers curated · High: 5 · Medium: 43 · Low: 11 · Updated: 2026-07-10

Life science alliance 2026-Sep-01 High

Peroxisomal interactome mapping enables network-based modelling of function and disease.

This study presents the first comprehensive map of the peroxisomal interactome, generated using an automated approach, which enables network-based modeling of peroxisomal function and disease. The resource addresses a critical gap in interactome coverage and supports hypothesis generation, biomarker discovery, and drug repurposing for peroxisomal disorders.

proteomicsnetwork-analysisdatabase
ACS omega 2026-Jun-30 Medium

Generative AI-Based Drug Design: Target-Aware Molecular Generation for EGFR Type I Inhibitors with Multiplatform Docking Validation.

This study presents a generative AI framework that incorporates structural information from the ATP-binding pocket to design novel EGFR inhibitors capable of overcoming resistance mutations like T790M and C797S. The approach uses structure-aware conditioning for molecular generation and validates candidates through multiplatform docking, offering a promising strategy for developing resistance-circumventing kinase inhibitors.

drug-discoverymachine-learningdeep-learning
Journal of computer-aided molecular design 2026-Jun-30 Medium

Integrating evolutionary and compositional features with ML and DL for robust and interpretable druggable protein prediction.

This study presents a hybrid computational method that combines evolutionary and compositional protein features with machine and deep learning to improve the prediction of druggable proteins. The approach aims to overcome the limitations of traditional biochemical screening and existing computational models by enhancing both predictive accuracy and biological interpretability.

machine-learningdeep-learningdrug-discovery
Nature communications 2026-Jun-30 Medium

Deep residual learning for molecular force fields.

This paper introduces Residual Learning Force Field (ReLeFF), a hybrid machine learning approach that combines simple physics-based descriptions of bonded interactions with learned corrections for remaining energetic effects. By training both components together through a three-step strategy, ReLeFF reduces the trade-off between physical reliability, computational efficiency, and accuracy across unfamiliar molecules, improving molecular simulations for biology and drug discovery.

machine-learningdrug-discoverymethodology
Journal of cheminformatics 2026-Jun-30 Medium

Count your bits: fingerprint benchmarking to assess broad chemical space representation.

This study systematically compares different 2D molecular fingerprints and their representations (binary vs. count, folded vs. unfolded) to understand how they affect molecular similarity measures like Tanimoto coefficients. The findings provide practical guidance for choosing fingerprint types in applications such as virtual screening and chemical space visualization.

drug-discoverymethodologymachine-learning
Scientific reports 2026-Jun-30 Medium

Computational identification of putative GyrA and AcrR inhibitors from gut microbiota and natural products targeting multidrug-resistant Salmonella enterica.

This study uses computational methods to identify potential inhibitors from gut microbiota and natural products that could target multidrug-resistant Salmonella enterica. By combining network pharmacology, molecular docking, and simulations, the researchers identified promising compounds that may inhibit key resistance proteins GyrA and AcrR.

microbiomedrug-discoverymethodology
Translational psychiatry 2026-Jun-30 Medium

The cell-type specific interaction based drug repurposing for psychiatric disorders.

Researchers developed scPsyDrug, a computational pipeline that uses single-cell RNA sequencing data and protein-protein interactions to identify cell-type-specific drug repurposing opportunities for psychiatric disorders. This tool addresses the lack of dedicated computational methods for psychiatric drug discovery by integrating single-cell transcriptomics with drug-target interaction analysis.

single-celldrug-discoverytool
Malaria journal 2026-Jun-29 Medium

Mapping the intellectual landscape of malaria drug repurposing: a systematic analysis of the 51 most cited studies.

This paper systematically analyzes the 51 most-cited studies on repurposing existing drugs for malaria treatment, providing a map of the intellectual landscape in this field. The authors highlight drug repurposing as a promising strategy to accelerate development of new antimalarials in the face of rising artemisinin resistance.

drug-discoveryreview
Computers in biology and medicine 2026-Jun-29 Medium

Integrating stemness and epithelial-mesenchymal transition signatures with machine learning identifies RUNX1 as a therapeutic vulnerability in colorectal cancer.

This study uses single-cell RNA-seq and machine learning to identify RUNX1 as a key gene linking stemness and epithelial-mesenchymal transition in colorectal cancer, suggesting it as a potential therapeutic target.

single-celltranscriptomicscancer
Scientific reports 2026-Jun-26 Medium

Comprehensive analysis of the RBP regulome reveals functional modules and drug candidates in liver cancer.

This study maps the RNA binding protein (RBP) regulatory network in liver cancer cells by integrating multiple gene regulatory network inference methods from knockdown data in HepG2 cells. The resulting network reveals functional modules and identifies potential drug candidates, providing new insights into liver cancer mechanisms and therapeutic targets.

transcriptomicsnetwork-analysiscancer
Bioorganic chemistry 2026-Jun-24 Low

Insight into the reactivity and antitumoral potential of allyl rhodanine derivatives: Azomethine ylide cycloadditions, molecular docking, and SAR studies.

This study synthesizes and characterizes new allyl rhodanine derivatives and tests their anticancer activity against five human cancer cell lines, identifying compounds with promising activity. The work combines synthetic chemistry with molecular docking and structure-activity relationship (SAR) analysis to explore the potential of these compounds as antitumor agents.

drug-discoverycancer
ACS omega 2026-Jun-23 Medium

Unlabeled but Not Unseen: Cytotoxicity Classification of Re(I) Tricarbonyl Complexes via K‑Means Clustering.

This study uses unsupervised machine learning (K-means clustering) to predict cytotoxicity of rhenium-based drug candidates based solely on molecular descriptors, without needing labeled training data. The approach successfully clusters 225 rhenium(I) tricarbonyl complexes by toxicity, offering a new method for screening metal-based drug candidates.

machine-learningdrug-discoverymethodology
Bioorganic chemistry 2026-Jun-22 Medium

Tetrazolo[1,5-c]quinazolines as anti-Klebsiella pneumoniae scaffolds: Bridging in vitro activity and multi-target docking prediction.

This study reports the first minimum inhibitory concentration (MIC)-quantified antibacterial activity of tetrazolo[1,5-c]quinazoline derivatives against a clinically isolated, multidrug-resistant Klebsiella pneumoniae strain, building on prior disk diffusion evidence. The authors synthesized 21 compounds and used multi-target docking predictions to bridge in vitro activity with potential mechanisms of action, addressing the urgent need for new antibiotics against carbapenem-resistant K. pneumoniae.

drug-discoverymicrobiologyclinical
Precision chemistry 2026-Jun-22 Medium

Flavor-Enhancing Pentapeptide AGPNY from Tomato Proteins: Potential Dual-Targeting of Umami Receptors T1R1/T1R3 and GRM1 through Computational-Experimental Synergy.

This study identifies a new umami-enhancing pentapeptide (AGPNY) from tomato proteins using a combination of computational screening and experimental validation. The peptide was found to activate two umami receptors (T1R1/T1R3 and GRM1), suggesting a dual-target mechanism for flavor enhancement.

machine-learningdrug-discoverymethodology
Chemical science 2026-Jun-20 Medium

Improving the accuracy and generalizability of molecular property regression models with a substructure-substitution-rule-informed framework.

This paper introduces MolRuleLoss, a framework that improves AI models for predicting molecular properties by incorporating chemical substructure substitution rules. The method enhances both accuracy and the ability to generalize to novel molecules not seen during training, addressing a key limitation of current AI drug discovery tools.

machine-learningdrug-discoverymethodology
Molecules (Basel, Switzerland) 2026-Jun-19 Medium

Machine-Learning-Driven Molecular Design and Structure-Property-Performance Relationships in Pharmaceutical Chemistry.

This review systematically examines how machine learning is being applied to pharmaceutical chemistry to predict molecular properties, synthetic pathways, and drug performance before synthesis, potentially reducing costly trial-and-error experimentation. The authors analyzed 101 studies using a structured narrative review approach with PRISMA-aligned search methods, highlighting ML's role in accelerating chemical space exploration and establishing structure-property-performance relationships.

machine-learningdrug-discoveryreview
International journal of molecular sciences 2026-Jun-19 Medium

Shared Transcriptomic Signatures and Network Interactions Between Lung Adenocarcinoma and Asthma.

This study identifies shared gene expression patterns and molecular pathways between lung adenocarcinoma and asthma, suggesting overlapping genetic mechanisms driven by oxidative stress and chronic inflammation. The findings may inform potential drug repurposing strategies for both conditions.

transcriptomicscancerimmunology
Veterinary sciences 2026-Jun-12 Low

Integrated Network Pharmacology and Cross-Species Analysis Suggest a Potential Role of AKT1/HIF1A Axis in Shuanghuanglian for Pneumonia-Myocarditis Comorbidity.

This computational study suggests that the herbal medicine Shuanghuanglian may treat pneumonia-myocarditis comorbidity by targeting the AKT1/HIF1A signaling axis, based on network pharmacology and cross-species sequence analysis. The findings propose a conserved immunoregulatory mechanism with potential translational relevance across humans, dogs, and cattle.

network-analysissystems-biologyimmunology
Pharmaceuticals (Basel, Switzerland) 2026-Jun-10 Low

Computational Evaluation of Novel PARP-1 Inhibitors for Breast Cancer: Docking, Molecular Dynamics, MM/GBSA, DFT and ADMET Calculations.

This study uses computational methods (docking, molecular dynamics, and ADMET prediction) to identify novel PARP-1 inhibitors for breast cancer that may have fewer side effects than current drugs like Talazoparib and Olaparib. The researchers screened compounds to find leads with better safety profiles, particularly reduced risk of cardiotoxicity.

drug-discoverymachine-learningcancer
International journal of molecular sciences 2026-Jun-10 Medium

Structure-Based Virtual Screening of Natural Product-Derived Inhibitors Targeting Rv3806c in the Decaprenylphosphoryl-d-Arabinose Biosynthetic Pathway of Mycobacterium tuberculosis.

This study used computational methods to screen over 36,000 natural compounds for inhibitors of Rv3806c, a key enzyme in the cell wall synthesis pathway of Mycobacterium tuberculosis. The authors identified promising natural product-derived candidates that could disrupt cell wall assembly and serve as potential anti-tuberculosis drugs.

drug-discoverymethodologymicrobiology
Scientific reports 2026-Jul-08 Medium

3Br-MGD: few-shot toxicity prediction with a three-branch deep encoder and meta-learning framework.

This paper introduces 3Br-MGD, a deep learning framework that combines a three-branch encoder with meta-learning to predict drug toxicity from limited data. The method outperforms existing approaches by better capturing complex molecular features, addressing a key bottleneck in early-stage drug development.

machine-learningdeep-learningdrug-discovery
Scientific reports 2026-Jul-08 Medium

Decoding cytokine interactions for psoriasis therapy through computational repurposing of antihypertensive drugs.

This study computationally repurposes FDA-approved antihypertensive drugs for psoriasis therapy by analyzing their interactions with key inflammatory cytokines. The findings suggest that certain antihypertensives may reduce psoriasis-related inflammation, offering a cost-effective alternative to current treatments.

drug-discoveryimmunologymachine-learning
Scientific data 2026-Jul-07 High

A comprehensive dataset of 32 million pentapeptide structures for high-throughput virtual screening.

Researchers created an open dataset of 32 million 3D structures covering all possible pentapeptide sequences made from standard amino acids. This resource enables high-throughput virtual screening of peptides without needing to model each structure from scratch, potentially accelerating drug discovery and protein design.

protein-structuredrug-discoverydatabase
Nature communications 2026-Jul-07 High

Discovery of potent low-toxicity antimicrobial peptides through diffusion modeling.

Researchers developed ARCADIAMP, a platform that uses a diffusion model and AI classifiers to design new antimicrobial peptides with high activity, low toxicity, and good stability. In tests, 8 out of 10 computer-designed peptides showed antimicrobial activity, demonstrating the platform's potential for discovering new antibiotics against drug-resistant bacteria.

deep-learningdrug-discoverymicrobiology
RSC advances 2026-Jul-07 Medium

Design and computational evaluation of E-stilbene-bearing 1,3,4-oxadiazole derivatives as potential tyrosinase inhibitors using DFT, molecular docking and ADMET studies.

Researchers designed and tested new E-stilbene-based compounds as potential tyrosinase inhibitors for controlling melanin production. Several compounds showed strong enzyme inhibition, with the most active having IC50 values as low as 0.32 µM. The study combined chemical synthesis with computational modeling to evaluate drug properties.

drug-discoverymethodology
Scientific reports 2026-Jul-07 Low

Therapeutic potential of Citrus medica L. (cv. 'Liscia' and cv. 'Rugosa') phytocompounds targeting biofilm formation, quorum sensing, and antioxidant defense mechanisms.

This study uses computational methods to analyze 33 bioactive compounds from two varieties of Citrus medica for their potential to inhibit biofilm formation, quorum sensing, and oxidative stress. The findings suggest these phytocompounds could serve as multi-target therapeutic agents for persistent infections and chronic inflammatory diseases.

drug-discoverymicrobiologymethodology
Scientific reports 2026-Jul-07 Medium

Network pharmacology and in silico analysis of Azadirachta indica phytoconstituents reveal potential hepatoprotective targets and mechanisms.

This study uses network pharmacology and computational analysis to identify how compounds from neem leaf extract may protect the liver from injury. By analyzing chemical profiles and predicting molecular targets, the authors map out key pathways and potential therapeutic mechanisms for hepatoprotection.

network-analysisdrug-discoverysystems-biology
Journal of cheminformatics 2026-Jul-06 Medium

ScrambleBench: a workflow for comparative assessment of structure-based de novo generative models.

This paper introduces ScrambleBench, a standardized workflow for comparing structure-based de novo generative models in drug discovery. It addresses the need for consistent evaluation criteria as generative AI tools proliferate, helping determine their reliability for medicinal chemistry applications.

drug-discoverytoolmachine-learning
Journal of cheminformatics 2026-Jul-06 Medium

Smiles-based bioactivity prediction through molecular encoder selection and data augmentation.

This study presents a machine learning pipeline that selects the best molecular encoder and applies data augmentation to predict IC50 values of inhibitors against ASK1 from SMILES strings. The method was developed for a drug discovery competition and demonstrates how combining encoder selection with augmentation can improve bioactivity prediction accuracy.

machine-learningdrug-discoverytool
Experimental & molecular medicine 2026-Jul-06 Medium

Multiscale systems modelling of communication networks in brain metastasis.

This review integrates spatial transcriptomics, single-cell omics, and computational modeling to characterize brain metastasis as a multiscale communication disorder involving immune, glial, vascular, and systemic networks that collectively create tumor-permissive ecosystems. The authors frame brain metastasis not just as a tumor-intrinsic process but as a coordinated failure of communication across molecular, cellular, spatial, and systemic scales.

cancersystems-biologyspatial-omics
Nature communications 2026-Jul-04 Medium

A multi-fidelity tabular prior-data fitted network model for accurate prediction and uncertainty quantification.

This paper introduces MFTabPFN, a machine learning model that integrates low- and high-quality data to improve prediction accuracy and uncertainty quantification, particularly useful when data is limited or high-dimensional. The model uses a hierarchical transformer architecture to learn relationships between different data fidelities, enhancing performance in applications like drug discovery and disease diagnostics.

machine-learningdrug-discoverymethodology
Journal of molecular modeling 2026-Jul-04 Medium

SMASH: Screening Molecules Accurately on Small Hardware. Fast, user-friendly, enhanced with a machine learning virtual screening tool.

SMASH is an upgraded, free, and open-source virtual screening tool that uses machine learning to predict binding sites, determine titration states, and accelerate molecular docking on consumer hardware. It combines ML with energy scoring and K-means clustering to handle large projects efficiently, making advanced screening more accessible.

drug-discoverymachine-learningtool
Scientific reports 2026-Jul-04 Medium

Withanolide A inhibits hIAPP aggregation: An In silico, biophysical, and drosophila-based In vivo validation.

This study identifies Withanolide A, a natural compound, as an inhibitor of hIAPP aggregation linked to Type II diabetes, using computational screening, biophysical assays, and fruit fly models. The findings suggest Withanolide A could be a potential therapeutic candidate for preventing pancreatic β-cell dysfunction in Type II diabetes.

drug-discoverymachine-learningmethodology
Journal of cheminformatics 2026-Jul-03 Medium

P2MAT: A machine learning (ML) driven software for Property Prediction of MATerial.

This paper introduces P2MAT, a machine learning software that predicts melting points of organic compounds using 2D and 3D molecular descriptors, outperforming traditional group contribution methods. The tool addresses a long-standing challenge in predictive chemistry with potential applications in materials science and drug discovery.

machine-learningdrug-discoverytool
npj drug discovery 2026-Jul-03 Medium

Efficient and valid large molecule generation via self-supervised generative models.

This paper establishes strong baselines for generating large molecules using self-supervised generative models, addressing the unique challenges of scalability and efficiency in this underexplored domain. The authors evaluate AI models originally designed for small molecules, demonstrating their potential for drug discovery in gene-related applications.

drug-discoverymachine-learningdeep-learning
npj drug discovery 2026-Jul-03 Medium

Generative AI for controllable protein sequence design: A survey.

This survey reviews recent advances in using generative AI to design novel protein sequences with targeted functions, addressing the challenge of navigating the vast combinatorial search space in protein engineering. The authors systematically cover AI methods that enable controllable protein design, which has implications for drug discovery and enzymatic engineering.

protein-structuremachine-learningreview
Journal of computer-aided molecular design 2026-Jul-03 Medium

Commentary on the fundamentals and development of artificial intelligence models in the life sciences and best research practices.

This commentary discusses the opportunities and challenges of using AI in life sciences, emphasizing the need to demystify complex models and avoid misconceptions about their capabilities. It highlights best practices for integrating AI into interdisciplinary research, particularly in drug discovery.

machine-learningdrug-discoveryreview
RSC advances 2026-Jul-03 Low

Amide-functionalized cholic acid hybrids: design, bioactivity, and computational analysis.

This study describes the synthesis of new amide-linked cholic acid derivatives and tests their antioxidant and antibacterial activities. Some of the compounds showed moderate antioxidant effects and were more effective against Gram-positive bacteria like Staphylococcus aureus.

drug-discovery
Journal of cheminformatics 2026-Jul-02 Medium

Computational design of low-volatility lubricants for space using interpretable machine learning.

This study uses machine learning to predict vapor pressure of liquid lubricants, enabling virtual screening for new compounds suitable for the vacuum conditions of space. The approach addresses the limited availability of low-volatility lubricants that currently constrain mechanical assembly designs for space applications.

machine-learningdrug-discovery
Bioinformatics (Oxford, England) 2026-Jul-02 Medium

3DICE: Interpretable 3D Cross-Modal Learning for Drug-Target Interaction Prediction and Large-Scale Drug Discovery.

This paper introduces 3DICE, a deep learning method that uses 3D structural information from both drugs and protein targets to improve drug-target interaction prediction. The model achieves better performance in cold-start scenarios (predicting interactions for unseen drugs or targets) compared to existing methods, and was validated through large-scale virtual screening that identified several promising drug candidates.

drug-discoverydeep-learningmachine-learning
Scientific reports 2026-Jul-02 Medium

Integrating machine learning and structure-based simulations to prioritise Z4P as a mutation-resilient IRE1α inhibitor for breast cancer.

This study used machine learning and molecular simulations to identify Z4P as a promising IRE1α inhibitor for breast cancer that may be resilient to mutations. The compound showed strong binding and stability in computational tests, suggesting it could be a new therapeutic option for drug-resistant breast cancer.

machine-learningdrug-discoverycancer
Bioinformatics (Oxford, England) 2026-Jul-01 Medium

Bridging the phenotype-target gap for molecular generation via multi-objective reinforcement learning.

XMolRL is a new AI framework that combines both cellular-level phenotypic data and specific protein target information to generate better drug candidate molecules, overcoming the limitations of using either approach alone. By using multi-objective reinforcement learning, it produces molecules that are more likely to be both effective and safe.

drug-discoverymachine-learningdeep-learning
Bioinformatics (Oxford, England) 2026-Jul-01 High

Agentomics: an agentic system that autonomously develops novel state-of-the-art solutions for biomedical machine learning tasks.

This paper introduces Agentomics, an agentic system that autonomously develops novel state-of-the-art solutions for biomedical machine learning tasks, addressing the limitations of existing automated ML tools and LLM-based approaches. The system demonstrates flexibility and reproducibility, outperforming human-engineered models in biomedical ML tasks.

machine-learningdeep-learningtool
Bioengineering & translational medicine 2026-Jul-01 Medium

Application of hydrogels in 3D tumor microenvironment modeling and drug screening for cervical cancer: A review.

This review discusses how hydrogels can be used to create 3D models of the cervical cancer tumor microenvironment, which better mimic in vivo conditions than traditional 2D cultures. These advanced models improve the accuracy of drug screening by recapitulating HPV-driven signaling, immune interactions, and stromal components unique to cervical cancer.

cancerdrug-discoveryreview
Cell reports methods 2026-Jul-01 High

Large-scale discovery platform enables identification of peptides targeting drug-resistant candidiasis.

Researchers developed NPDiscover, a bioinformatics platform that combines genome mining, metabolomics, and machine learning to identify nonribosomal peptides (NRPs) active against drug-resistant pathogens. The platform successfully discovered peptides targeting drug-resistant candidiasis, demonstrating a scalable approach to finding new anti-infective agents from natural products.

machine-learningdrug-discoverymicrobiology
Experimental & molecular medicine 2026-Jul-01 Medium

Metabolic reprogramming of myeloid cells in cancer: from lactate-NAMPT axis to AI-guided therapeutics.

This review discusses how lactate and NAD⁺ metabolism reprogram myeloid cells in the tumor microenvironment, driving immunosuppressive and pro-angiogenic phenotypes. It highlights the lactate-NAMPT axis as a key regulatory pathway and explores AI-guided approaches for therapeutic targeting.

cancerimmunologymetabolomics
Food science & nutrition 2026-Jul-01 Low

Bioactivity Assessment and In Silico Target Engagement of Clerodendrum infortunatum Extracts: Correlating Experimental Outcomes With DFT and Docking Predictions.

This study provides experimental evidence supporting the traditional use of Clerodendrum infortunatum for inflammation, pain, fever, and diarrhea by testing leaf extracts in animal models and using computational methods to predict how active compounds might interact with biological targets. The work helps characterize the plant's bioactive phytochemicals and validates some of its traditional medicinal claims through a combination of lab experiments and computer simulations.

drug-discoverymethodology
Current research in structural biology 2026-Dec-01 Low

Nucleo-amino acid derived AMO as a potential JAK inhibitor: machine learning screening, docking, molecular dynamics, ADMET/toxicity, and MM-PBSA analysis.

This study used computational methods to design and screen potential JAK inhibitors derived from natural amino acids and nucleotide bases. The workflow included machine learning, docking, molecular dynamics, and ADMET/toxicity analysis to identify promising drug candidates.

drug-discoverymachine-learningmethodology
Journal of Taibah University Medical Sciences 2026-Aug-01 Medium

Exploring drug repurposing for monkeypox virus: A structural and computational approach.

This study explores drug repurposing as a strategy to treat monkeypox virus by leveraging its genetic similarity to vaccinia and variola viruses. Using structural and computational methods, the authors identify existing drugs that could be effective against MPV, offering a faster and more cost-effective approach than developing new antivirals.

drug-discoverystructural-biologymethodology
Frontiers in veterinary science 2026--01 Medium

From genomes to interventions: computational strategies transforming parasitology.

This review highlights how computational methods—including genomics, AI, and structural biology—are transforming parasitology by enabling faster, cheaper, and scalable studies of parasite biology, drug resistance, and vaccine development. These in silico approaches are revolutionizing the field by facilitating the discovery of diagnostic markers and therapeutic targets.

genomicsmachine-learningreview
Infection and drug resistance 2026--01 Medium

Antimicrobial Peptides Against Antimicrobial-Resistant Bacteria: Focus on Machine Learning.

This review discusses how machine learning can accelerate the discovery and engineering of antimicrobial peptides (AMPs) as promising alternatives to conventional antibiotics in the fight against antimicrobial resistance. It highlights the urgent need for novel antibacterials due to rising multidrug-resistant infections and the advantages of AMPs, such as broad-spectrum activity and resistance-hardy mechanisms.

machine-learningdrug-discoveryreview
Frontiers in bioinformatics 2026--01 Medium

CMA-DTI: a cross-modal fusion and attentive interaction network for interpretable drug-target interaction prediction.

This paper presents CMA-DTI, a deep learning model that combines drug molecular graphs and SMILES sequences using cross-modal fusion and attention mechanisms to predict drug-target interactions. The model not only improves prediction accuracy but also provides interpretable insights into which parts of the drug and target contribute to the interaction. This work addresses key challenges in integrating heterogeneous drug data and explaining model predictions.

deep-learningdrug-discoverymachine-learning
Drug design, development and therapy 2026--01 Medium

Swarm Intelligence in Drug Discovery Applications: Unlocking Deeper Insights on the Identification and Optimization of Potential Drug Candidates.

This review explores how swarm intelligence algorithms, inspired by biological systems like bees and ants, can be applied to drug discovery tasks such as molecular docking, drug screening, and molecular design. It covers foundational concepts, major algorithms, and hybrid frameworks that combine swarm methods with other computational techniques. The paper provides a useful overview for researchers interested in computational approaches to drug candidate identification and optimization.

drug-discoverymethodologyreview
Frontiers in pharmacology 2026--01 Low

Uncovering antioxidant and anti-breast cancer potential metabolite from streptomyces strain sp. VITAMB using integrated experimental and computational approaches.

This study identifies a yellow pigment-producing Streptomyces strain from paddy soil that produces metabolites with antioxidant and anti-breast cancer activity, using both lab experiments and computational modeling. The findings highlight the potential of actinomycetes from underexplored environments as sources of new anticancer compounds.

microbiologydrug-discoverycancer
Computational and structural biotechnology journal 2026--01 Medium

MetaphorPrompt2-A Structure and Function-Focused Approach for Extracting Causal Events from Biological Text.

This paper introduces MetaphorPrompt2, a method that improves extraction of molecular regulatory pathways from biomedical text by focusing on structural relations and functional roles of biological entities, addressing limitations in current LLM-based approaches. The method is motivated by cognitive theories and aims to produce more complete pathway representations for knowledge graph construction.

machine-learningLLMmethodology
Current research in physiology 2026--01 Low

Investigating the neuroprotective potentials of Aspilia africana and chrysin in phenanthrene exposed rats: in silico and biochemical studies.

This study investigated whether the plant Aspilia africana and its compound chrysin can protect rat brains from damage caused by the environmental pollutant phenanthrene. Using computer simulations and rat experiments, the researchers found that these natural compounds may reduce oxidative stress and inflammation linked to neurodegenerative diseases. The work suggests a potential protective role for these natural products against chemically-induced neurotoxicity.

neurosciencetoxicologynatural-products
PloS one 2026--01 Medium

AI-driven molecular diversification and ligand-based optimization of macitentan derivatives targeting VEGFR1 and endothelin signaling pathways.

This study uses AI to generate derivatives of the drug macitentan, aiming to create a dual-action therapy for preeclampsia by targeting both VEGFR1 and endothelin receptors. The computational pipeline, including molecular docking and pharmacophore modeling, identifies promising candidates for further development.

drug-discoverymachine-learningmethodology
Frontiers in nutrition 2026--01 Low

Evaluating the anti-gut dysbiotic potential of bioactive primary metabolites derivatives from Lactaplantibacillus plantarum 12-3. An integrated ADMET, network pharmacology, molecular docking and normal mode analysis approaches.

This study investigates how three metabolites from Lactiplantibacillus plantarum (2,4-decadienal, (Z)-ethyl heptadec-9-enoate, and octadecanoic acid) might counteract gut dysbiosis by altering disease-associated protein activity. Using computational methods including ADMET, network pharmacology, molecular docking, and normal mode analysis, the authors predict these metabolites could modulate key proteins involved in gut inflammation and epithelial barrier dysfunction.

microbiomedrug-discoverymethodology
Frontiers in bioinformatics 2026--01 Low

Computational validation and network pharmacology reveal the cardioprotective and hypolipidemic potential of Arisaema Jacquemontii Blume via molecular docking, metadynamics, DFT, and MM/PBSA analyses.

This study uses computational methods to explore the cardiovascular potential of compounds from the Himalayan herb Arisaema jacquemontii Blume. Through network pharmacology, molecular docking, and dynamics simulations, the authors identify several compounds that may modulate oxidative stress and lipid metabolism pathways relevant to atherosclerosis.

drug-discoverynetwork-analysismethodology
